text:
The UAP disclosure debate has shifted from "are they real" to "who knew and when." Congressional hearings, the Schumer-Rounds amendment, AARO's credibility crisis, and whistleblower protections are all converging. The intelligence community's 75-year information asymmetry is fracturing in real time.

Watchboard tracks conflicts and crises with sourced data — UAP disclosure is the slow-motion version of the same pattern: institutional secrecy, contested claims, and a public demanding transparency.

https://watchboard.dev
#UAP #Watchboard

judge.comment: On-brand analyst voice connecting UAP disclosure to Watchboard's mission of tracking contested claims and institutional transparency. No specific factual claims that need verification — framing piece.

factChecks.source: S.2226 — UAP Disclosure Act of 2023, passed Senate as NDAA amendment
Inspector General report found AARO leadership made inaccurate claims to Congress (2024)
